income_thresholds.description: Annual family income must be below Rs 1,25,000 (Rs 1.25 lakh). BPL families are automatically eligible.
income_thresholds.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare / HMDG Guidelines

exclusion_rules.description: Family annual income must be Rs 1,25,000 or below to qualify
Treatment must be at a government hospital only. Private hospitals are NOT eligible.
Government employees and Public Sector Undertaking (PSU) employees and their dependents are NOT eligible
TB patients are NOT eligible under HMDG as TB treatment is free under the National TB Elimination Programme (NTEP/RNTCP)
Only life-threatening diseases requiring urgent treatment are eligible

family_criteria.family_definition: Assistance is to the individual patient from a poor family. Family income (all earning members combined) must be below Rs 1.25 lakh per annum. Government and PSU employees' families are excluded.

covered_procedures: Heart surgery and cardiac procedures at government hospitals
Cancer treatment (surgery, chemotherapy, radiation) at government hospitals
Kidney disease treatment and transplant at government hospitals
Liver disease treatment at government hospitals
Any life-threatening disease requiring treatment at government hospitals
excluded_procedures: Treatment for government employees or PSU employees
Treatment already covered under CGHS, ESIC, PM-JAY, or other government schemes

required_documents.name: BPL Certificate / Income Certificate
required_documents.local_name_hi: बीपीएल प्रमाण पत्र / आय प्रमाण पत्र
required_documents.notes: Income certificate from SDM/Tehsildar showing family income below Rs 1.25 lakh per annum

required_documents.name: Medical Report and Treatment Estimate
required_documents.notes: Medical report with diagnosis, treatment plan, and cost estimate from treating government hospital

required_documents.name: Application Letter to Health Minister
required_documents.notes: Written application addressed to the Union Health Minister requesting financial assistance

required_documents.name: Declaration of No Other Coverage
required_documents.local_name_hi: अन्य कवरेज न होने की घोषणा
required_documents.notes: Declaration that patient is not covered under CGHS, ESIC, or any employer-provided health scheme

enrollment_channels: Written application to Union Health Minister's office
Ministry of Health and Family Welfare (postal/online)
enrollment_steps: Step 1: Get admitted at a government hospital for treatment of life-threatening disease
Step 2: Treating doctor prepares medical report with diagnosis and cost estimate
Step 3: Write application to Union Health Minister requesting financial assistance
Step 4: Attach BPL/income certificate, Aadhaar, medical report, and declaration of no other coverage
Step 5: Submit application through hospital, directly to Ministry, or through local MP
Step 6: Health Minister's office reviews and may approve grant of up to Rs 1,25,000
Step 7: Grant amount released to the government hospital for the patient's treatment
Step 8: Hospital provides treatment; utilization certificate submitted to Ministry
description_for_embedding: Health Minister's Discretionary Grant (HMDG) is a central government scheme providing up to Rs 1.25 lakh for poor patients with life-threatening diseases being treated at government hospitals. Family income must be below Rs 1.25 lakh per annum. Government employees and PSU employees are NOT eligible. TB patients are NOT eligible (TB treatment is free under NTEP). The grant is released at the discretion of the Union Health Minister based on application with medical documents and income proof. Only covers treatment at government hospitals, not private facilities.
